title: rawhide: podman run -> exit 127
body:
The last two podman rawhide builds have failed gating tests. Looks like a real issue:
```
# podman run alpine date      [note the lack of output]
# echo $?
127
```
podman-1.6.4-0.30.dev.git6392477.fc32.x86_64
conmon-2.0.3-0.1.dev.gitbc758d8.fc32.x86_64
crun-0.10.6-1.fc32.x86_64
runc-1.0.0-129.dev.git2186cfa.fc32.x86_64

The f3f219a build was good; c673ff8 was bad. c673ff8 is the only commit since a good build, and it's a test-only PR (unlikely to be causing this problem), so there's something else going on. I'm continuing to look into it. Publishing issue to get more eyes on it.

[debug.log](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3877030/podman-run.debug.log)

comments.body: Downgraded to `crun-0.10.5-2.fc32` and `conmon-2.0.2-0.1.dev.git422ce21.fc32`; no change. Cannot downgrade kernel (`5.4.0-0.rc0.git3.1.fc32.x86_64`) on this vm nor can I easily get a vm with a different kernel.

It's an AVC:
```
type=AVC msg=audit(1574380012.187:1499): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=21249 comm="date" path="/lib/ld-musl-x86_64.so.1" dev="vda1" ino=266990 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c29,c643 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:var_lib_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1574380012.187:1500): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=21249 comm="date" path="/bin/busybox" dev="vda1" ino=266829 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c29,c643 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:var_lib_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
```
Downgrading from `container-selinux-2:2.120.1-0.2.dev.gita233788.fc32` to `2.120.1-0.1.dev.git6fb6dcf.fc32` resolves the problem. Filed https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/issues/83 - not filing rhbz because the last few container-selinux builds have all been RH Container Bot builds, so I would expect the bot to pick up a build if this gets fixed.
This is a labeling issue on your container storage.
